metrics.narrative: Bass Pro voiced specific, quantifiable pain: catalog/punch-out search surfaces non-preferred vendors ahead of their own ~55 punch-outs (Whitney Parks, 19 Mar 2026), and Coupa's redesigned spend-analysis module is unusable enough that category managers 'aren't using it anymore' and revert to ad hoc reporting (Whitney Parks, 19 Mar 2026). During the 30 Mar 2026 demo, Zycus's own AI-driven spend analysis of Bass Pro data surfaced a $1.3B consolidation opportunity among the top 5 suppliers, but this is a Zycus-generated figure that has not been buyer-validated or tied to a formal ROI case against the ~$445K investment.

sources: Avoma commercial call, 29 Jun 2026 (Next_Step__c, notes only -- recording unavailable)
champion.narrative: David Elford has been the most consistent buyer-side driver -- requesting detailed pricing breakdowns, pushing for a 5-year term, requesting references, and attending the 29 Jun 2026 commercial call. But his advocacy has been uneven: he asked that outreach 'move under his direction' (22 Jun 2026 Next Step), which held Zycus back from broader executive contact, and the same note records over a week of buyer unresponsiveness before a reply arrived on 25 Jun 2026. He is not the economic buyer, and there is no evidence he has advocated for Zycus beyond his own function -- accounts payable and executive sign-off both remain outstanding by his own account.

competition.narrative: Coupa is Bass Pro's 6-year incumbent with a renewal cadence every 3 years and next contractual renewal targeted Q1/Q2 2027 (discovery call, 12 Mar 2026) -- which removes any incumbent-contract-expiration urgency. Buyer dissatisfaction with Coupa is real and specific, but Coupa is actively defending the account: the 29 Jun 2026 Next Step flags 'RISK - coupa is going onsite wednesday,' which computes to on or around 1 Jul 2026 (~8 days ago, now past). No outcome of that visit is captured in any source read -- a genuine unresolved knowledge gap, not a settled risk.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well-documented and buyer-voiced across three separate calls: three ERPs feeding Coupa via manual overnight flat-file integration and a disconnected legacy Cabela's CLM tool (12 and 19 Mar 2026); Coupa's slower support response since its Thoma Bravo PE acquisition (12 Mar 2026); and poor catalog/punch-out prioritization that surfaces Coupa's own preferred vendors ahead of Bass Pro's ~55 preferred punch-outs (Whitney Parks, 19 and 30 Mar 2026). This pain is specific, repeated, and consistent.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, Order Form, legal redline, or InfoSec questionnaire appears in any Task, Next Step, or Avoma record through 9 Jul 2026. Implementation was estimated on 6 Apr 2026 as 'year one, or up to 18 months,' with professional-services fees framed as a one-time fee at roughly 1.1-1.3x software cost across 4-6 milestone payments -- but none of this has become a drafted document. With 21 days to the close date, this is the largest mechanical risk regardless of vendor selection.

economic_buyer.narrative: No CFO or CPO has attended any of the six discovered touchpoints (12 Mar-29 Jun 2026), and none appears on the Opportunity Contact Roles. David Elford (Director, Non-Merchandise Procurement) has led the process throughout but said plainly on 6 Apr 2026 that he would need to 'socialize this with a bunch of other internal folks that would need to sign off,' including accounts payable. The 22 Jun 2026 rep Next Step names the CFO and David's boss as the escalation targets if David stayed unresponsive, and the 29 Jun 2026 Next Step records an offer of an executive-to-executive session with senior Zycus leadership, but there is no evidence Bass Pro accepted or that a CFO/CPO conversation has occurred. This is the single largest structural gap in the deal.

decision_process.narrative: On the 27 May 2026 T&E demo a Bass Pro attendee asked directly whether this evaluation was formal or would become an RFP; no buyer-side answer was captured in the notes, so the process type remains unconfirmed. David Elford said on 6 Apr 2026 that internal socialization (~2 weeks) with accounts payable and a then-open replacement hire would precede deeper module review, and the 29 Jun 2026 Next Step records the buyer stating a decision was needed 'in the coming week or so' (implying roughly by 6 Jul 2026) -- a target that has now passed (3 days ago) with no confirmed stage movement.

decision_criteria.narrative: Bass Pro drove a detailed scoping exercise: transparent per-module pricing, one-pagers for iSource/iContract/iSupplier (David Elford, 6 Apr 2026), and confirmed licensing assumptions ($250M P2P of $1.04B total spend, 1,500 procurement/intake users, 5 power users iSource, 10 iContract, 10 iSupplier, 1,000 contracts, 225,000 invoices). Microsoft Teams-integrated intake (Merlin Intake) and preserving the Egencia travel-booking relationship inside a bundled Emburse T&E module were explicitly prioritized (27 May 2026 T&E demo). No formal weighted scorecard or written RFP has surfaced in any source read.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 23 (30 Jul 2026). Created 12 Mar 2026, 117 days old. Time in Shortlisted: 89 days since 6 Apr (last stage touch inferred from 6 Apr proposal review call). No qualified_date in SF. No stage progression to Vendor Selected or Negotiation despite 29 Jun commercial call and pricing approval. Deal needs vendor selection by 13 Jul + references + MSA/SOW/legal/InfoSec (6-8 weeks compressed to 2.5 weeks) to close 30 Jul. Date will slip to Sep/Q3 unless selection happens this week and contracting starts immediately. Forward slip from 30 Jul implied. Buyer confirmed 29 Jun 'they need to make a decision in the coming week' — decision window is 6-13 Jul 2026. Coupa onsite 3 Jul (29 Jun Next Step: 'coupa is going onsite wednesday') introduces late-stage competitive risk at the exact moment Bass Pro must choose. LastActivityDate 30 Jun (7 days ago) shows recent engagement. Buyer email received 25 Jun (12 days ago) confirms two-way dialogue. Rep outreach 24 Jun (voicemail + text to David and Whitney) awaiting buyer reply. Deal is LIVE but compressed timeline + competitive escalation + missing economic buyer + no references completed = high execution risk. Pace required: vendor selection this week, references week of 14 Jul, MSA/SOW draft + legal + InfoSec parallel-tracked to close 30 Jul — achievable only if buyer commits immediately and Coupa onsite does not flip the decision.
evidence: Pricing well received on 29 Jun commercial call (Next Step 29 Jun: 'Pricing well received'). Buyer understands urgency: 'they need to make a decision in the coming week or so' (Next Step 29 Jun). This frames a decision window of 6-13 Jul 2026.

Coupa escalating with onsite visit this week (29 Jun Next Step: 'RISK- coupa is going onsite wednesday' — 3 Jul 2026). Coupa is the incumbent with 2,000 entrenched P2P users (6 Apr call: David said 'we've got 2,000 people who are used to doing this a certain way'), zero implementation cost, and a renewal path requiring no change management. Coupa likely offering pricing concessions or executive sponsorship to block the switch at the exact moment Bass Pro must decide. This is a head-to-head late-stage bakeoff with Coupa holding the defensive advantage.

Economic buyer (CFO or CPO) not engaged 24 days from close. David Elford (Director, Non-Merchandise Procurement) is project lead but not budget owner. He stated 6 Apr 'we're gonna have to socialize this with a bunch of other internal folks that would need to sign off before we moved at all' and 'one of the biggest ones… is our accounts payable team.' Next Step 22 Jun confirms escalation to 'CFO and CPO/David's boss' was contemplated if no response. No CFO or CPO on OpportunityContactRole, no exec on any Avoma call. Without EB sponsorship, the deal cannot close or will be pushed past 30 Jul.

No quantified ROI or business case to justify $500k implementation vs. Coupa renewal. Pricing was well received 29 Jun, but no CFO/budget owner has validated the investment. David's concern 6 Apr was 'change management, 2,000 people used to doing it a certain way' — this pain has not been translated into dollar savings, headcount reduction, or cycle-time improvement. Coupa can offer a renewal with zero implementation cost and zero change management, making status quo the path of least resistance unless Zycus quantifies the business case this week.

No reference calls completed despite buyer request 6 Apr. David asked for 'two or three references… P2P customers who were previous Coupa customers… to hear their thoughts on the transition, how their organization adapted, and what change management was involved.' This is the proof point that de-risks David's change-management concern. No evidence references have been set up or completed in Next Steps, tasks, or call notes. Without references, the buyer cannot validate the change-management risk, and Coupa's 'no change' renewal pitch wins.

Close date 30 Jul (24 days) is not credible without immediate vendor selection, references, and contracting kickoff. Even if Zycus is selected 13 Jul, the deal needs references (1 week), MSA/SOW draft and negotiation (2-3 weeks), legal redlines (1-2 weeks), InfoSec review (SOC 1/2, DPA — 1-2 weeks), and ERP integration scoping (1 week) to close 30 Jul. This compresses 6-8 weeks of contracting into 2.5 weeks. Field history shows no recent stage progression, and deal is still in Shortlisted with no evidence of Vendor Selected, Negotiation, or Contract In Progress. The close date will slip to Sep or Q3 unless vendor selection happens this week (by 13 Jul) and contracting starts immediately with milestone commitments.

customer_preference.evidence: David Elford said on 30 Mar 2026, after the demo, that if Bass Pro undertook the change 'we'd go all the way to the right to the full platform,' and pricing was reported 'well received' on 29 Jun 2026 -- but no buyer has stated a final vendor decision, and Coupa remains an active, low-friction renewal option.

competitive_position.summary: Bass Pro is running a two-vendor evaluation (Zycus vs. Coupa renewal) with Coupa holding the defensive advantage as the 6-year incumbent with 2,000 entrenched P2P users, zero implementation cost, and a Feb 2027 renewal window that allows retention without forcing a switch. Coupa escalated competitive pressure 29 Jun by scheduling an onsite visit for 3 Jul 2026 — a late-stage retention play timed exactly when Bass Pro must decide (decision window 6-13 Jul). This is a head-to-head bakeoff at the moment of truth. Coupa's wedge is 'no change, no risk, no cost' — they can offer continuity with familiar workflows, no training burden, no implementation expense, and a 30-day renewal vs. Zycus's 6-18 month implementation. Zycus's wedge is Microsoft Teams-native Merlin Intake + unified S2P analytics + better search/catalog UX, directly addressing Bass Pro's stated pains (fragmented intake, cumbersome Coupa analytics, poor search). However, Zycus has not converted the wedge into a quantified ROI or secured CFO/CPO engagement, leaving the business case qualitative and vulnerable to Coupa's status-quo pitch. Coupa's weaknesses (poor analytics UX, slow support, no intake) are confirmed and articulated by Whitney Parks and David Elford across calls, but Coupa can counter by promising product roadmap improvements and pricing concessions onsite 3 Jul. The single strongest CURRENT threat is Coupa's onsite visit 3 Jul: if Coupa secures CFO/CPO commitment or shifts the timeline during that meeting, the deal flips to Coupa renewal or slips past 30 Jul close. Zycus has 6 days (until 13 Jul decision window closes) to complete reference calls, book the exec alignment meeting (Zycus RVP + Bass Pro CFO/CPO), and counter Coupa's pitch with a quantified business case and exec sponsorship. Without these moves this week, Coupa's 'no change' renewal is the path of least resistance. Threat level: HIGH and rising. Status: Coupa is active, escalating, and holds the inside track on retention. No other competitors in play.

hard.ais_why:
• AI importance is logged as 'Neutral' and the agentic-platform discussion, Gen AI commentary and AI-RFP rating fields are all blank. The Merlin product line of Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake is Zycus-side tagging rather than a reflection of documented buyer asks.
• On the 89-minute March 30 commercial meeting with Whitney Parks, Kory Cooper and David Elford, the buyer's action items were strictly pricing-oriented: pricing templates, P2P invoice counts, contract volumes, T&E partner details, and module-based pricing options across P2P, P2P+Sourcing and P2P+Spin Analytics. AI, autonomous negotiation and Merlin did not surface once.
• All four captured meetings between March 12 and April 6 focus on P2P scoping, pricing and support model questions. With an explicit 'Neutral' AI posture and no buyer-voiced AI signal across nearly three hours of recorded discussion, this opportunity sits firmly in Resistant territory.

Complete reference calls this week (Coupa-to-Zycus customers) to prove transition ROI and change management. Quantify business case (hours reclaimed, maverick spend eliminated, cycle time improvement) and present to budget owner to justify implementation vs. renewal cost. Deliver updated commercial proposal with Emburse T&E total cost. Activate the wedge: unified platform (one code base, no bolt-ons), Teams integration (no new software, adoption advantage), AI intake/analytics (solves Coupa's cumbersome UI pain), and change management support (workshops, 24x5 service desk). Close this week before Coupa's onsite visit shifts the buyer's decision timeline.
